title: ssotdeps cross-check guards pending vs implemented constraint drift

evidence.ref: ssot-dependency-map.riido.json carried Cross-repo vocab scan in pending and planned:true on both vocablint links from D014 through D021 (drift survived 8 decisions)
014-vocablint — actually shipped vocablint to both siblings; the dep map's pending entry has been false since this decision merged
019-cleanup-c002-guard-status — same SSOT-honesty class as D022 but human-only cleanup; D022 adds the mechanical guard

guards.ref: .github/workflows/contracts.yml — Cross-check dep map pending vs concept-map status (decision 022)
internal/ssotdeps/ssotdeps_test.go TestCrossCheckFlagsImplementedPending — covers the exact pre-cleanup drift case plus nil-tolerance and false-positive guards

examples: Before D022: pending=['Cross-repo vocab scan (constraint C-001 enforcement).'] and C-001 status=implemented → cross-check returns 1 stale reference, CI red. After D022 cleanup: pending no longer references C-001 → cross-check returns 0, CI green.
If a future constraint C-NNN flips to implemented but the author forgets to clear a pending entry mentioning it, CI fails immediately rather than letting the dep map drift for weeks.
counterexamples: Pending entry that mentions a constraint id which is still pending (status=pending) is OK — that's the legitimate use of pending[].
Pending entry with no constraint reference (e.g. 'Generation links for backend GraphQL schema and frontend client.') is unaffected — no C-XXX mention to cross-check.
